summary: @solana-labs/etherjs ships install-time host-info exfiltration via postinstall script
details: Package name impersonates the official Solana Labs scope while shipping an install.js that runs automatically via package.json's postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js"). On install, the script reads host identity (os.hostname(), process.platform), invokes execSync and curl, and performs HTTP/HTTPS POST requests carrying the collected data off-host. The package has no legitimate library content matching its advertised purpose; the install script is the payload. Installing this package on any developer or CI machine causes immediate disclosure of host identifiers and command output to a remote endpoint and provides the publisher with a foothold to deliver further payloads.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
